title: Grounds for Disciplinary Action Against Principals/Employers and
content: 42 Penalties. Commission by a foreign principal/employer of any of the offenses below shall 43 be a ground for the imposition of the corresponding penalty. 44 45 I. SERIOUS OFFENSES. Serious offenses are punishable by permanent disqualification 46 and delisting from the roster of accredited principals/employers: 47 48 a. Gross violation of laws, rules, and regulations on overseas employment; 49 50 b. Gross negligence leading to serious injury or illness or death of the seafarer; 51 52 c. Grave misconduct against the seafarer; 53 54 d. Non-payment or underpayment of wages and benefits; 55 56 e. Commission of any act by the principal/employer or its agent or representative 57 against the seafarer, which is tantamount or amounts to a felony, crime, or offense 58 punishable by the laws of the Philippines or by the port state/flag state; 59 60 f. Compelling a seafarer to work for another employer or perform another work; 61 47 g. Passing on or deducting from a seafarer’s salary the payment of the cost of 1 insurance fees, premium, or other insurance related charges as provided under 2 the compulsory insurance coverage; 3 4 h. Deliberate concealment or failure to inform the LMA of significant events adversely 5 affecting the welfare, well-being, status, or condition of its hired seafarers; 6 7 i. Abandonment of Seafarers, as defined under Part I, Rule II, Item 1 herein; or 8 9 j. Unlawful transfer of seafarers to another vessel without approval from the 10 Department. 11 12 II. LESS SERIOUS OFFENSES. Less serious offenses are punishable by Six (6) Months 13 to one (1) year suspension from participation in the overseas employment program, or in 14 lieu thereof, the alternative penalty of fine may be imposed as prescribed under the DMW 15 Adjudication Rules and Procedures and R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR: 16 17 a. Default on its other contractual obligations to the seafarer and/or to its manning 18 agent; 19 20 b. Substitution of the DMW-approved contract; 21 22 c. Withholding of the seafarer’s travel and other pertinent documents; 23 24 d. Violation of other laws, rules and regulations on recruitment and placement; 25 26 e. Failure or refusal to present employment records, such as payroll and daily time 27 records, pay slips, or other documents, such as medical records, that belong or 28 pertain to the seafarer and that is in the possession of the foreign 29 principal/employer, when required by the individual seafarer, the Secretary of the 30 DMW, the Secretary of the DOLE, their duly authorized representatives, or by the 31 duly authorized representative of a recognized organization provided that the 32 maximum fine that may be imposed for this offense shall not exceed Php 33 1,000,000.00, pursuant to R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR; 34 35 f. Failure to monitor and submit quarterly reports on the welfare, well-being, status 36 or condition relating to its hired seafarer to the Department; 37 38 g. Failure to monitor and submit reports within five (5) calendar days from being 39 informed of the incident on significant or critical incidents to the Department; 40 41 h. Failure to provide periodic bi-monthly reports of the action taken to the seafarer’s 42 family or next of kin and the DMW as applicable until the matter is resolved, in 43 relation to the significant or critical incidents; or 44 45 i. Any other violation of the rights of the seafarers provided under R.A. No. 12021 46 and its IRR. 47 48 49 Disciplinary Action Against Seafarer, Classification of Offenses and Penalties 50 51
